stories.title: Iran war hinders the flow of U.N. aid through the Gulf to communities in need
stories.description: It's not just oil tankers stranded near the Strait of Hormuz. U.N. aid shipments are also hindered by the war in Iran.

stories.title: With strikes above and crackdowns on the ground, Iranians describe life under siege
stories.description: A state-imposed internet blackout has obscured the reality of life in Iran as the war rages on. Those fleeing through neighboring countries share a rare glimpse of what life is like in Iran.

stories.title: Author Scott Anderson on Iran's power structure after death of top security official
stories.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with journalist and author Scott Anderson about Iran's power structure after the killing of Ali Larijani, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council.

stories.title: War can't entirely eliminate Iran's nuclear program, the U.N. atomic energy chief says
stories.description: International Atomic Energy Agency head Rafael Grossi said Iran's nuclear program is heavily damaged, "but the material will still be there and the enrichment capacities will be there."

stories.title: Cheap drones are reshaping modern warfare — and catching the U.S. off guard
stories.description: As Operation Epic Fury enters its third week, relentless attacks by cheap Iranian drones are being fended off by multi-million-dollar U.S. interceptors. How long can the math hold up?

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Iranians fleeing their country share rare glimpse of what life is like amid war, Trump floats idea of "taking" Cuba, the Federal Reserve is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady.

stories.title: Once the airstrikes stop, Iran's nuclear threat leaves no easy endgame
stories.description: Even after airstrikes end, Iran's nuclear threat looms and diplomacy may be too late.

stories.title: Trump says US 'knew nothing' about Israel's attack on major Iran gas field
stories.description: Qatar reports "extensive damage" at an energy industry site as Iran retaliates for gas field attack.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
stories.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

stories.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: Ayman Mhanna: 'Lebanon has become the theatre for a war between Israel and Iran'
stories.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Ayman Mhanna, Executive Director of the Beirut-based Samir Kassir Foundation. The war between Iran and Israel is unfolding in Lebanese territory. While Lebanon demonstrates notable competence in distributing humanitarian aid, coordinating with i...

stories.title: Live: Iran intensifies strikes on Gulf energy plants after 'extensive' damage at Qatari gas hub
stories.description: Iran intensified its attacks on its Gulf Arab neighbours' energy infrastructure Thursday, setting Qatari liquified natural gas facilities ablaze as it hit back following an Israeli attack on its main natural gas field, a major escalation in the Middle East war that has sent globa...

stories.title: International shipping 'chaos' as Strait of Hormuz closure sends ripple effects across the globe
stories.description: Around 90% of global goods are transported by sea. And while just approximately 5% of that travels directly through the Strait of Hormuz, Iran's effective closure of the key passage is having a ripple effect, disrupting trade routes world wide. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks w...

stories.title: Qatar orders departure of two Iranian diplomats over gas facility attack
stories.description: Qatar's foreign ministry on Wednesday ordered Iran's military and security attaches along with their staff to leave the country within 24 hours, following an attack on its massive natural gas facility. FRANCE 24's Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha.

stories.title: Are US soldiers really "refusing orders" to fight in Iran war?
stories.description: More than two weeks after the US and Israel launched military strikes on Iran, a rumour has sprung up that American soldiers are refusing, en masse, to fight. There is no evidence for it. However, the number of conscientious objectors in the US military is on the rise, according ...

stories.title: Israel kills Iran’s intelligence chief as both sides ratchet up attacks on energy facilities
stories.description: Iran suffered major blows on Wednesday with the killing of its intelligence minister, Esmail Khatib, in an Israeli strike and an attack on the South Pars natural gas field, one of the world's largest gas resources and a pillar of the country's energy supplies. Tehran vowed retali...

stories.title: US seeks to curb soaring petrol prices
stories.description: The Trump administration is taking steps to curb rising energy costs, as it continues to wage war in the Middle East. The US is easing sanctions on Venezuela, and suspending a century-old shipping law, in an effort to get more oil supplies into the global market. Oil prices conti...

stories.title: 'Acute risk' that Iraq could become once again victim of internal conflict as Iran war progresses
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Yinka Oyetade, Zaid Al-Ali, Visiting Fellow at Princeton University and Former UN Advisor in Iraq, explains that until the war in Iran broke out, Iraq had been able to "straddle between US-aligned interests and Iranian-aligned interests" but there's an "...

stories.title: Iran did not try to rebuild enrichment after June 2025 attack, US intel chief says
stories.description: Iran made no effort to rebuild uranium enrichment after its capabilities were destroyed in a June 2025 US-Israeli attack, US intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard testified Wednesday, contradicting President Donald Trump's justifications for his ongoing war. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson...

stories.title: Both sides in Iran war ratchet up attacks on energy facilities, as oil prices surge
stories.description: Saudi Arabia intercepted and destroyed four ballistic missiles that were launched ​toward Riyadh on Wednesday, the Defence Ministry said, just ahead of a meeting of regional and Islamic foreign ministers in the Saudi capital. FRANCE 24's Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha.

stories.title: Trump's dire strait: No strategy, no endgame, no allied support?
stories.description: Today in the Middle East, Day 19 of the war, there have been extensive air strikes, drone attacks and missiles launched. And fall-out is spreading far beyond the so called theatre of war. And the funeral of Ali Larijani, Iran's de facto leader, key advisor to the Ayatollah, the h...

stories.title: 'Not a one-person regime': Why Iran's Islamic Republic is so hard to topple
stories.description: Since the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on February 28, US and Israeli strikes have decimated the regime's top ranks, from the commander of the Revolutionary Guards, to the country's security and intelligence chiefs. But even as the heads of the Iranian ...

stories.title: Trump attempts to distance US from Israeli strikes on key Iranian gasfield
stories.description: Questions raised over US knowledge of Israeli plans to attack key Iranian gasfield as energy sites become targets.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 20 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: The Iran war enters day 20 as Israeli and Iranian attacks hit energy infrastructure across region.

stories.title: Iran executes three convicted of killing police in recent pre-war unrest
stories.description: Those executed were allegedly involved in the killing of two law enforcement personnel.

stories.title: Attacks on GCC show ‘IRGC don’t think, they just push a button’
stories.description: Khaled Batarfi, a Saudi political analyst, says Iran’s attacks on the GCC are irrational and create anger.

stories.title: Asia’s stock markets dive after attacks on energy facilities in Qatar, Iran
stories.description: Stocks tumble as attacks by Israel, Iran on critical energy infrastructure exacerbate fears for global energy supplies.

stories.title: Tehran rescue workers pull survivors from rubble
stories.description: Video released by Iran's Red Crescent shows emergency services rescuing survivors of US-Israeli strikes on Tehran.

stories.title: Could oil hit $200 a barrel? Analysts no longer think it is far-fetched
stories.description: Prices are likely to rise substantially if the Strait of Hormuz remains, in effect, closed, market watchers say.

stories.title: Three Palestinian women killed during Iranian missile attack in West Bank
stories.description: Women killed, 13 injured when debris from a missile, or possibly a cluster bomblet, hit a beauty salon near Hebron.

stories.title: ‘Does America have a plan? Israel has a plan. Does America know?’
stories.description: Daniel Levy, says that the US-Israel war on Iran is reckless and harms allies for Israel's benefit.

stories.title: Three women killed in occupied West Bank missile attack
stories.description: Three women in an occupied West Bank hair salon became the first Palestinian deaths of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

stories.title: Saudi FM warns Iran that patience in Gulf not ‘unlimited’ amid attacks
stories.description: Foreign minister warns Iran that regional neighbours have 'significant' capabilities with which to respond to attacks.

stories.title: Iran war live: Qatar, Saudi energy sites attacked; Riyadh says trust gone
stories.description: Qatar says missile attack caused fires and extensive damage at Ras Laffan as Saudi Arabia warns Iran of action.

stories.title: Qatar expels Iranian attaches after LNG facility strike
stories.description: Qatar is expelling Iran’s military and security attaches after Iranian missiles caused “extensive damage” to Ras Laffan.

stories.title: Ministerial meeting calls on Iran to ‘immediately and unconditionally’ halt attacks in Gulf
stories.description: A ministerial meeting, hosted by Saudi Arabia, called on Iran to “immediately and unconditionally” halt attacks against Gulf countries in the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, the Saudi Arabian Foreign Ministry said on Thursday. The conflict began with US-Israeli strikes on Ir...

stories.title: Gabbard’s missile claim ‘not grounded in strategic reality’, says former foreign minister
stories.description: Former caretaker foreign minister Jalil Abbas Jilani on Thursday dismissed a statement made by US Dire­ctor of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard over Pakistan’s missile capabilities posing a threat to the United States. In a post on X, the former foreign minister maintained tha...

stories.title: Israeli attack on largest gas field turns Middle East into powder keg
stories.description: • Tehran threatens, launches retaliatory attacks on energy facilities in the region• UAE, Qatar slam Tel Aviv attack on South Pars field, which Israel claims was approved by Washington• Pezeshkian warns of ‘uncontrollable consequences’ as oil stays above $106 per barrel• World ec...

stories.title: Controversial Iranian scientist selected for ‘Nobel prize of water’
stories.description: ISLAMABAD: A controversial Iranian political figure and exiled architect of the ‘water bankruptcy’, Kaveh Madani, has been selected for ‘Stockholm Water Prize’ amid the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran for transforming his groundbreaking research into global policy, diplomacy and ou...

stories.title: Saudi Arabia to celebrate Eid tomorrow
stories.description: RIYADH: Saudi Arabia has announced that Eidul Fitr would be celebrated on Friday, according to a statement published by state media. “The Supreme Court has decided that tomorrow, Thursday, is the completion of the 30th day of the month of Ramazan, and that Friday is the day of th...

stories.title: US efforts against terrorism in the spotlight after Kent’s exit
stories.description: • Ex-counterterrorism chief lost his wife — a naval officer — in Syria in 2019, completed 11 combat tours• Represents MAGA’s anti-war faction THE top US counterterrorism official, who resigned from his role to protest the war against Iran, is under the spotlight for his anti-war ...

stories.title: Germany ‘would have advised against’ war on Iran, says Merz
stories.description: BERLIN: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said on Wednesday that if the United States had consulted Berlin about the US-Israel war against Iran, “we would have advised against taking this course of action”. “We share with Israel and the United States the objective that Iran should...

stories.title: Pakistan missiles ‘significant threat’ to US: Gabbard
stories.description: NATIONAL Intelligence Director Tulsi Gabbard testifies along with FBI, DIA and CIA directors during a Senate intelligence committee hearing on worldwide threats.—AFP • Director of National Intelligence places Pakistan alongside Russia, North Korea; believes their projectiles coul...

stories.title: ‘Free France’: Macron reveals name of Europe’s largest warship
stories.description: President Emmanuel Macron on Wednesday announced that France’s next nuclear-powered aircraft carrier will be called “France Libre” (Free France), as the country looks to reinforce its status as a major maritime power. Once completed, the warship, which is set to replace the count...

stories.title: Anthropic AI an ‘unacceptable risk’ to military, US govt says
stories.description: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic posed an “unacceptable risk” to military supply chains, the US government insisted on Tuesday, as it defends against the tech firm’s challenge to a designation as dangerous. Anthropic’s Claude AI model has been in the spotlight in recent ...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
